name: Pennsylvania
tagline: A first-in-the-nation government AI program, a $90B+ wave of AI data-center investment, and a $1.16B broadband buildout — Pennsylvania is one of the most active SLG technology markets in the country.

execSummary.summary: Pennsylvania is one of the most dynamic SLG technology markets in the country right now, and three forces define it. First, the Shapiro Administration ran the nation's first state-government generative-AI pilot (ChatGPT Enterprise with OpenAI and Carnegie Mellon) and in April 2026 expanded AI to 3,000+ employees across 35 agencies under a new Generative AI Governing Board — agencies are actively operationalizing AI and need governed infrastructure. Second, a roughly $90B wave of AI data-center investment is landing in the Commonwealth, anchored by Amazon's $20B commitment and the $10B Homer City gas-powered campus, making PA national-scale AI infrastructure. Third, the central technology buy runs through the Department of General Services' COSTARS cooperative program and statewide contracts, with the Office of Administration (OA-OIT) setting enterprise IT and security policy. The 2025–26 budget landed late ($50.1B, signed Nov 12, 2025), and 2026–27 negotiations are live now — timing matters for forecasting.
opportunities: AI-infrastructure / 'AI factory' demand tied to the $90B+ PA data-center boom (PowerEdge, networking, services) plus university research computing.
Governed AI for state agencies expanding from 3,000 toward enterprise scale — private/on-prem options for sensitive workloads under the new AI Governing Board.
NIST-aligned endpoint and security refresh as SB 377 pushes NIST standards into IT procurement.
Client, endpoint, and lifecycle modernization across ~80,000 executive-branch employees and the CODE PA digital-services push.

budget.intro: Pennsylvania budgets annually on a July 1 fiscal year. The 2025–26 General Fund budget of $50.1B was signed November 12, 2025 — 135 days late — and 2026–27 negotiations are underway now. There is no single 'state IT budget' line; technology spend is distributed across agencies, the Office of Administration's enterprise IT services, and named digital and broadband initiatives.
notes: The 2025–26 budget was enacted 135 days late; confirm appropriation status before forecasting against agency timelines.
BEAD is federal infrastructure money administered by the PA Broadband Development Authority, not General Fund IT spend — different buyers and rules.

FY2027 (governor's proposed, per NASBO): $53.3B general fund, +5.4% vs FY2026; $137.22B all funds. Proposes drawing $4.58B from the rainy day fund to offset a negative projected balance, leaving a $3.31B reserve.

initiatives.name: Statewide Generative AI Expansion
initiatives.summary: Pennsylvania ran the first state-government gen-AI pilot (ChatGPT Enterprise with OpenAI and Carnegie Mellon's Block Center); pilot employees saved ~95 minutes a day. In April 2026 the Shapiro Administration expanded AI to 3,000+ employees across 35 agencies, with 6,500 more in training, under a new Generative AI Governing Board. A clear opening to lead with governed, private/on-prem AI for sensitive workloads as adoption scales.

initiatives.name: AI Data-Center & Energy Buildout
initiatives.summary: A ~$90B investment wave is landing in PA: Amazon's $20B commitment (incl. a campus beside the Susquehanna nuclear plant), the $10B Homer City gas-powered AI campus on a retired coal site, and CoreWeave's $6B Lancaster build. PA's natural-gas and nuclear power plus its RGGI exit are pitched as energy advantages. The strongest tailwind for Dell ISG / AI-infrastructure demand in the state.

initiatives.name: BEAD Broadband Deployment
initiatives.summary: PA's $1.16B BEAD program (via the PA Broadband Development Authority) closed its final application round in August 2025; the board provisionally approved $793.4M in grants pending NTIA sign-off. Aimed at universal high-speed access by decade's end. Mostly a fiber/ISP motion, but it signals sustained network and edge investment statewide.

initiatives.name: SB 377 — NIST Standards in IT Procurement
initiatives.summary: SB 377 (Phillips-Hill) passed the Senate 49-0 in June 2025, requiring procured endpoint devices and related services to meet NIST cybersecurity guidelines or industry best practices. It pushes a NIST baseline into Commonwealth IT contracting — favorable to vendors who can document NIST-aligned endpoint security and lifecycle controls.

procurement.intro: The Department of General Services (DGS) runs central procurement, including the COSTARS cooperative purchasing program ('Cooperative Sourcing to Achieve Reductions in Spend'), which lets registered local governments, school districts, and state-affiliated entities buy from DGS-established contracts — including IT hardware and software — without running their own RFP. The Office of Administration (OA-OIT) sets enterprise IT strategy, security policy, and shared services for the ~80,000 executive-branch employees under the Governor's jurisdiction.

vehicles.name: COSTARS Cooperative Purchasing
vehicles.detail: Primary cooperative vehicle via DGS for local gov, schools, and state-affiliated entities. Covers IT hardware/software. Confirm Dell agreements and reseller registration are current.

vehicles.name: DGS Statewide Contracts
vehicles.detail: Pre-competed Commonwealth contracts for IT commodities and services used by executive agencies.

vehicles.name: OA-OIT Enterprise IT Services
vehicles.detail: Shared infrastructure, security policy, and IT operations for executive-branch agencies — sets standards a winning offer must meet.

entities.name: Office of Administration (OA-OIT)
entities.role: Enterprise IT strategy, security, and shared services; home of the CIO and gen-AI program.

entities.name: Department of General Services (DGS)
entities.role: Central procurement and COSTARS cooperative — the statewide buying motion.

entities.name: Department of Human Services / large agencies
entities.role: Among the largest agency IT and data-modernization spenders.

entities.name: Penn State, PASSHE & state-related universities
entities.role: Large, largely independent research-computing and AI-infrastructure buyers.

procurement.competition: Pennsylvania is a contract-driven market where COSTARS and DGS statewide contracts shape access and incumbents hold positions across compute, client, and networking. Differentiation tends to come from AI-infrastructure capability tied to the data-center wave, NIST-aligned security (SB 377), and governed AI for agencies rather than price alone.

powerStructure.name: Josh Shapiro
powerStructure.title: Governor
powerStructure.note: Champions the gen-AI program and the AI data-center/energy agenda; drove the RGGI exit as an energy play.

powerStructure.name: Bry Pardoe
powerStructure.title: Commonwealth CIO
powerStructure.note: Named CIO Oct 2025; former CODE PA executive director. Owns enterprise IT and cybersecurity direction.

powerStructure.name: Neil Weaver
powerStructure.title: Secretary of Administration
powerStructure.note: Leads OA (HR, IT, records) for ~80,000 employees; public face of the AI governance push.

powerStructure.name: PA Broadband Development Authority (PBDA)
powerStructure.title: Broadband program owner
powerStructure.note: Administers the $1.16B BEAD program and grant awards.

news.title: PA expands generative AI to 3,000+ employees across 35 agencies
news.impact: Agencies are scaling AI under a new Governing Board — demand for governed, secure AI infrastructure grows.

news.title: Gov. Shapiro proposed a $53.3B general-fund FY2027 budget
news.impact: $137.22B all funds, +2.2% vs FY2026; proposes a $4.58B rainy-day draw, leaving a $3.31B reserve. Recommends environmental and community protections for responsible data-center development and calls on agencies to create guidance for safe AI use.

news.title: Shapiro signs $50.1B 2025–26 budget, 135 days late
news.impact: Budget finally enacted; late cycle compresses agency procurement timelines and sets up live 2026–27 talks.

news.title: CoreWeave secures funding for $6B Lancaster AI data-center campus
news.impact: Adds to PA's national-scale AI-infrastructure footprint.

news.title: Amazon commits $20B to PA data centers (incl. site by Susquehanna nuclear)
news.impact: Largest private investment in state history; cements PA as an AI-infrastructure hub.

news.title: SB 377 (NIST standards in IT procurement) passes Senate 49-0
news.impact: Pushes a NIST cybersecurity baseline into Commonwealth IT contracting.

news.title: Homer City retired coal plant to become $10B gas-powered AI campus
news.impact: Signals PA's energy-advantage pitch for hyperscale AI compute.

dellActions: Lead the AI-infrastructure conversation: tie Dell ISG / AI Factory offerings to PA's ~$90B data-center wave and to Penn State / PASSHE research-computing buyers.
Position governed, private/on-prem AI for sensitive agency workloads as the gen-AI program scales past 3,000 users under the new Governing Board.
Lead endpoint and security refresh with NIST-aligned lifecycle controls to align with SB 377's procurement direction.
Validate Dell's COSTARS and DGS statewide-contract coverage and reseller registration are current for the agencies and locals you're targeting.
Time agency engagement to the budget cycle — the 2025–26 budget was late and 2026–27 talks are live; confirm appropriations before forecasting.
